questions.prompt: What is a common role of a load balancer?

answers.text: Health checks can prevent routing to unhealthy instances.

feedback.legit: Correct. Health checks can prevent routing to unhealthy instances.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Health checks can prevent routing to unhealthy instances.

answers.text: Distribute incoming requests across healthy service instances.

feedback.legit: Correct. Distribute incoming requests across healthy service instances.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Distribute incoming requests across healthy service instances.

answers.text: A load balancer routes traffic; it is not the primary database.

feedback.legit: Correct. A load balancer routes traffic; it is not the primary database.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. A load balancer routes traffic; it is not the primary database.

questions.prompt: Why is a cache placed in front of a slower data source?

answers.text: To serve repeated reads with lower latency and less backend load.

feedback.legit: Correct. To serve repeated reads with lower latency and less backend load.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. To serve repeated reads with lower latency and less backend load.

answers.text: To guarantee that data can never become stale.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Caches need an explicit freshness and invalidation strategy.
feedback.sus: Correct. Caches need an explicit freshness and invalidation strategy.

answers.text: A cache miss still requires access to the underlying source.

feedback.legit: Correct. A cache miss still requires access to the underlying source.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. A cache miss still requires access to the underlying source.

answers.text: Automatic protection from every application bug.

feedback.legit: Not quite. A bad write can be replicated to every copy.
feedback.sus: Correct. A bad write can be replicated to every copy.

answers.text: A live replica is a complete substitute for an independent backup.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Replication can copy deletion or corruption, so recovery still needs an appropriate backup strategy.
feedback.sus: Correct. Replication can copy deletion or corruption, so recovery still needs an appropriate backup strategy.

answers.text: Additional copies that can improve availability or read capacity.

feedback.legit: Correct. Additional copies that can improve availability or read capacity.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Additional copies that can improve availability or read capacity.

answers.text: Cross-shard queries and transactions can become more complex.

feedback.legit: Correct. Cross-shard queries and transactions can become more complex.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Cross-shard queries and transactions can become more complex.

answers.text: It partitions data across multiple database nodes.

feedback.legit: Correct. It partitions data across multiple database nodes.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. It partitions data across multiple database nodes.

answers.text: The partition key determines placement and strongly affects balance.

feedback.legit: Correct. The partition key determines placement and strongly affects balance.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. The partition key determines placement and strongly affects balance.

questions.prompt: Why are idempotency keys useful for write APIs?

answers.text: They help repeated delivery avoid creating the same logical operation twice.

feedback.legit: Correct. They help repeated delivery avoid creating the same logical operation twice.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. They help repeated delivery avoid creating the same logical operation twice.

answers.text: They make network retries unnecessary.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Retries still occur; the key helps the server recognize duplicates.
feedback.sus: Correct. Retries still occur; the key helps the server recognize duplicates.

answers.text: The server must store or derive the result associated with a key.

feedback.legit: Correct. The server must store or derive the result associated with a key.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. The server must store or derive the result associated with a key.

questions.prompt: What does a durable message queue help decouple?

answers.text: It guarantees each message is processed exactly once without application logic.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Many queues use at-least-once delivery, so consumers should handle duplicates.
feedback.sus: Correct. Many queues use at-least-once delivery, so consumers should handle duplicates.

answers.text: A queue always preserves one global message order across all consumers.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Ordering guarantees depend on the queue type, partitioning, and consumer design.
feedback.sus: Correct. Ordering guarantees depend on the queue type, partitioning, and consumer design.

answers.text: Producers from consumers in rate and availability.

feedback.legit: Correct. Producers from consumers in rate and availability.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Producers from consumers in rate and availability.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of a circuit breaker?

answers.text: A half-open state can probe whether recovery has occurred.

feedback.legit: Correct. A half-open state can probe whether recovery has occurred.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. A half-open state can probe whether recovery has occurred.

answers.text: Stop repeated calls to a failing dependency for a period.

feedback.legit: Correct. Stop repeated calls to a failing dependency for a period.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Stop repeated calls to a failing dependency for a period.

answers.text: Repair the dependency automatically.

feedback.legit: Not quite. It limits cascading damage but does not fix the underlying service.
feedback.sus: Correct. It limits cascading damage but does not fix the underlying service.

answers.text: Capacity and fairness by restricting request volume over time.

feedback.legit: Correct. Capacity and fairness by restricting request volume over time.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Capacity and fairness by restricting request volume over time.

answers.text: It guarantees low latency after every accepted request.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Accepted work can still be slow for other reasons.
feedback.sus: Correct. Accepted work can still be slow for other reasons.

answers.text: Distributed rate limits require a strategy for shared state or approximation.

feedback.legit: Correct. Distributed rate limits require a strategy for shared state or approximation.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Distributed rate limits require a strategy for shared state or approximation.

questions.prompt: Why serve static assets through a CDN?

answers.text: A CDN makes cache invalidation unnecessary.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Changed content still needs versioning, expiry, or invalidation.
feedback.sus: Correct. Changed content still needs versioning, expiry, or invalidation.

answers.text: A CDN safely caches every origin response by default.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Cacheability depends on method, headers, status, and CDN configuration; private responses need particular care.
feedback.sus: Correct. Cacheability depends on method, headers, status, and CDN configuration; private responses need particular care.

answers.text: Edge locations can reduce latency and origin traffic.

feedback.legit: Correct. Edge locations can reduce latency and origin traffic.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Edge locations can reduce latency and origin traffic.

questions.prompt: What choice does the CAP theorem highlight during a network partition?

answers.text: Different operations can make different tradeoffs.

feedback.legit: Correct. Different operations can make different tradeoffs.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Different operations can make different tradeoffs.

answers.text: A distributed system cannot guarantee both linearizable consistency and availability for every request while the partition persists.

feedback.legit: Correct. A distributed system cannot guarantee both linearizable consistency and availability for every request while the partition persists.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. A distributed system cannot guarantee both linearizable consistency and availability for every request while the partition persists.

answers.text: A system must permanently choose only two of three properties even without failures.

feedback.legit: Not quite. The tradeoff concerns behavior while a partition prevents communication.
feedback.sus: Correct. The tradeoff concerns behavior while a partition prevents communication.

questions.prompt: What is backpressure in a data-processing system?

answers.text: A way for slower consumers to limit or signal upstream production.

feedback.legit: Correct. A way for slower consumers to limit or signal upstream production.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. A way for slower consumers to limit or signal upstream production.

answers.text: A requirement to buffer unlimited work in memory.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Unlimited buffering delays failure and can exhaust resources.
feedback.sus: Correct. Unlimited buffering delays failure and can exhaust resources.

answers.text: Dropping, batching, blocking, or shedding work are possible policies.

feedback.legit: Correct. Dropping, batching, blocking, or shedding work are possible policies.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Dropping, batching, blocking, or shedding work are possible policies.

answers.text: A single ACID transaction that locks every service database.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Sagas avoid one global database transaction across services.
feedback.sus: Correct. Sagas avoid one global database transaction across services.

answers.text: A compensating action automatically rewinds database history as if the earlier step never happened.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Compensation is new business logic and may not perfectly reverse every prior effect.
feedback.sus: Correct. Compensation is new business logic and may not perfectly reverse every prior effect.

answers.text: A saga guarantees globally serializable isolation across every participating service.

feedback.legit: Not quite. A saga coordinates local transactions but does not by itself provide one globally serializable transaction.
feedback.sus: Correct. A saga coordinates local transactions but does not by itself provide one globally serializable transaction.
